title: SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - 211info
text: EASTERN OREGON ALCOHOLISM FOUNDATION SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residential and outpatient treatment for adults with drug and/or alcohol addictions. Provides evaluations for people arrested for DUIs, Minor in Possession (MIP) information classes, two transitional living facilities (one for men, one for women and children), and alcohol- and drug-free apartments for those who have successfully completed treatment. The residential program includes a child development program for men, women, and their children. 216 SW Hailey Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who meet criteria for residential alcohol and drug addiction treatment. Mothers and single fathers referred by DHS Child Welfare may have children approved to reside with them. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 278-2558 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 278-2558 Medical Detoxification Center Main phone (541) 276-3518 Residential Recovery Center Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: Sliding scale fee. DUI fees are determined by the court. DOCUMENTS: Call for information AREA SERVED: Umatilla County SITE HOURS: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Services: Assessment for Substance Use Disorders Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment DUI Offender Programs Residential Substance Use Disorder Treatment Facilities Sober Living Homes If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
